ranked_movies.title: The Lobster
ranked_movies.reason: The original post that sparked the discussion. Highly recommended for its unique, dystopian premise that is described as hilarious, horrifying, and deeply thought-provoking.

ranked_movies.title: The Act of Killing
ranked_movies.reason: Extremely high engagement across multiple posts. Repeatedly recommended as a mind-blowing, unsettling, and impactful documentary that changes one's perspective.

ranked_movies.title: Three Identical Strangers
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned across multiple documentary request threads. Described as shocking, mind-blowing, and a must-watch.

ranked_movies.title: Logan
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as a movie that far exceeded expectations. Praised for its brutal action, great character development, and compelling story, standing out in the superhero genre.

ranked_movies.title: Irreversible
ranked_movies.reason: Heavily discussed as a genuinely good yet profoundly traumatizing film. Noted for its intentional use of sound and visuals to create a nauseating and unforgettable experience.

ranked_movies.title: The Killing of a Sacred Deer
ranked_movies.reason: Directly recommended as the next logical step after 'The Lobster'. Part of the Yorgos Lanthimos filmography, known for being weird, brilliant, and thought-provoking.

ranked_movies.title: Happiness
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times as a chilling 'comedy' with intersecting storylines. Described as a movie that will severely fuck you up with intention.

ranked_movies.title: The Jinx
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently recommended documentary series. Described as intense, eye-opening, and a must-watch for true crime fans.

ranked_movies.title: Requiem for a Dream
ranked_movies.reason: The catalyst for the trauma request post. The benchmark for a genuinely good movie that is also a horrifying and traumatic viewing experience.

ranked_movies.title: Kids
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as a movie that will shake you to your core. Its ending is described as being burned into one's memory.

ranked_movies.title: The Emperor's New Groove
ranked_movies.reason: Unexpected masterpiece. Discussed as one of Disney's best films that earned its love through video rentals after a troubled production and little promotion.

ranked_movies.title: Dogtooth
ranked_movies.reason: Another Yorgos Lanthimos film frequently mentioned in the same discussion as 'The Lobster'. Noted for being a favorite and a film that stays with you.

ranked_movies.title: Mysterious Skin
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as an extremely dark film that shakes you to your core, featuring prominent actors and heavy themes.

ranked_movies.title: Galaxy Quest
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a movie with very low expectations that blew viewers away. Celebrated for its humor and originality.

ranked_movies.title: Boogie Nights
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in the context of depicting the 70s porn industry. Recognized as a real film about the era when porn had plots, acting, and production value.

ranked_movies.title: The Thing
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted for a specific, iconic comedic moment in a serious scene, demonstrating its lasting cultural impact and memorability.

ranked_movies.title: Event Horizon
ranked_movies.reason: Noted for a perfectly delivered comedic line in the midst of unspeakable horror, making it a memorable and frequently quoted moment.

ranked_movies.title: The Magnificent Ambersons
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a major discussion about film preservation, studio interference, and the ethics of using AI to reconstruct lost footage, highlighting its historical importance.

ranked_movies.title: Watership Down
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a film that will severely fuck you up, indicating its powerful and traumatic impact on viewers.

ranked_movies.title: Welcome to the Dollhouse
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested alongside 'The Lobster' as a film with a similar quirky, dark, and impactful tone.
